headline: Maria’s growth plan is blocked by unresolved ambiguity from last cycle, and she’s parking energy in mentoring without a clear next decision trigger.
summary_bullets: Maria is still carrying the decision because “The feedback was not specific enough” and she needs “clearer scope” before she can commit.
Mentoring matters, but she can’t name what “would move the next decision,” so mentoring is currently growth without a measurable decision point.
She reports a drop in ritual motivation (“I am less motivated in team rituals lately”) while also stating an org-level impact goal—misalignment between aspiration and current confidence.
understanding_paragraph: Maria is not disengaged from growth; she’s stuck on the quality of the last-cycle feedback and what the gap actually was. She wants org-level impact, but she can’t translate that into a concrete “next decision” through her current mentoring path. The session clarified that her main blocker is scope specificity and decision clarity, not effort or interest.

axes.meaning: Weak signal: team-ritual motivation dipped, but there’s no clear evidence of sustained burnout.
Worth noting: engagement is down in team rituals, and she’s not yet leaning into a clear next move.
Clear gap: she explicitly needs “clearer scope” and still can’t operationalize the last-cycle readout.
Defining signal: she can name the target (“impact at org level”) but not the decision trigger, so growth momentum stalls.

brutal_truth_employee: Maria is stuck on specificity: “The feedback was not specific enough” and “I want clearer scope.” She also can’t commit to a next step because “I do not know what would move the next decision,” even though she says “Mentoring matters to me.”
brutal_truth_manager: When Maria said “I do not know what would move the next decision,” the conversation stayed at reflection and didn’t force a concrete decision trigger (what outcome, by when, and who decides). The moment to tighten was after “I want clearer scope”—that’s where you should have offered a specific scope draft and a decision point, not just asked for more thinking.

next_actions.action: Draft a one-page mentoring scope for Maria: target org-level outcome, mentee/stakeholder list, weekly cadence, and a single review decision for next month.
Confirm Maria’s last-cycle feedback readout with the decision owner and translate it into a concrete gap statement with examples she can use.
Run the 18-month question again with a forced choice: pick one org-level impact mechanism and name the next decision trigger tied to the mentoring scope.

watch_for: Within two weeks: if Maria can’t restate the mentoring scope as “X outcome by date Y with stakeholder Z,” the growth blocker is still unresolved.
Before next 1:1: if Maria’s team-ritual motivation (“less motivated in team rituals lately”) doesn’t improve after you clarify the scope, engagement won’t recover.
